behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-API-client handoff (request assembly + dispatch payload). A unit test of a helper could miss wiring/serialization omissions. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stub provider client that captures outbound requests. Invoke classify() with a known taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full taxonomy label list (no drops/reordering loss per contract) on every call path.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about handling provider output at the module boundary, so module+provider-adapter wiring should be exercised. Real infra is unnecessary; a stubbed provider response can reproduce the failure. LLM quality is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake provider that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and the raw unexpected label is never surfaced.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Unit because behavior text explicitly says "When the API returns..."; boundary handling is part of the requirement.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is a pure normalization determinism property on identical inputs. No cross-module interaction is required to expose the failure; no real infra or model call is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with identical raw provider payload fixtures (including casing/whitespace variants embedded identically per fixture) and assert deterministic identical final label every run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior text scopes to "normalization step" with identical raw input, i.e., local logic.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of model-returned labels. A stub would hide real model/provider variability; correctness depends on real model outputs over repeated runs.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real-provider classifications for the same document+taxonomy under stable config, recording raw provider responses and final labels. Assert final labels are unchanged across runs where raw provider response is unchanged; if raw response changes, allow corresponding label change.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because behavior text targets consistency of provider/model decisions, not only HTTP mechanics.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: The failure is often at handoff: budget logic must gate dispatch behavior (truncate/reject) correctly. In-process fake provider and tokenizer fixtures can catch this without real network/OS. No model-output-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget and policy. Invoke classify() with over-budget input using a fake provider client that tracks whether dispatch occurs and what payload is sent. Assert module applies configured truncate/reject policy before dispatch and never exceeds allowed pre-dispatch token count.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System: behavior text says "before dispatch" and can be validated without live provider.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider token accounting from a real AI API call; mocks can mask tokenizer/accounting differences that cause real overruns. Although assertion is deterministic, execution depends on real AI service behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classifications for representative documents against the real provider and capture provider-reported usage metadata. Assert reported total tokens per call are <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget report.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "Provider-reported token usage," which a stub cannot validate faithfully.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across module and provider client interface (error mapping + backoff + retry). This is reproducible with stubbed 429/rate-limit responses and controllable clock; no real OS/network required. Not dependent on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider returning rate-limit signal for first K attempts, success on K+1. Use controllable clock/sleeper abstraction to assert wait occurs between attempts and final result is returned instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because real HTTP timing is not required by behavior text; retry logic can be validated in-process.
